name: Binary search
summary: Finds a target in a sorted array by halving the search range each step.

name: Bubble sort
summary: Repeatedly swaps adjacent elements that are out of order until the array is sorted.

name: Merge sort
summary: Divides the array in half recursively, then merges sorted halves back together.

name: Quick sort
summary: Picks a pivot, partitions elements around it, then recurses on each partition.

name: Breadth-first search
summary: Explores a graph level by level using a queue — ideal for shortest paths.

name: Depth-first search
summary: Explores as far as possible down each branch before backtracking.

name: Dijkstra's algorithm
summary: Finds the shortest path from a source to all nodes in a weighted graph.

name: Dynamic programming (0/1 knapsack)
summary: Builds solutions to subproblems in a table to avoid recomputing overlapping work.

name: Two pointers
summary: Uses two indices moving toward each other to solve array problems in linear time.

name: Sliding window
summary: Maintains a window of elements that slides across an array to track a running result.

name: Floyd's cycle detection
summary: Uses fast and slow pointers to detect a cycle in a linked list in constant space.

name: Kadane's algorithm
summary: Finds the maximum sum subarray by tracking the best running sum seen so far.

name: Topological sort
summary: Orders nodes in a DAG so every directed edge goes from earlier to later in the order.

name: Union-find (disjoint set)
summary: Tracks connected components efficiently using path compression and union by rank.

name: Heap sort
summary: Builds a max-heap then repeatedly extracts the maximum to produce a sorted array.

name: Backtracking
summary: Incrementally builds candidates for a solution and abandons branches that fail constraints.

name: Trie (prefix tree)
summary: A tree of characters that enables fast prefix search and autocomplete operations.

name: Bellman-Ford algorithm
summary: Finds shortest paths from a source even when edges have negative weights.

name: Counting sort
summary: Sorts integers by counting occurrences — beats O(n log n) when the value range is small.

name: Kruskal's algorithm
summary: Builds a minimum spanning tree by greedily adding the cheapest edge that doesn't form a cycle.
